questions.question: If the machine temperature increases significantly, what is the most likely effect on machine health?

explanations.a: Higher temperatures generally reduce machine health.
explanations.b: Excessive temperature negatively affects machine condition.

questions.question: Which parameter is most useful for detecting bearing wear?

explanations.a: Humidity does not directly indicate bearing wear.
explanations.b: Temperature may rise but is not the primary indicator.
explanations.c: Vibration monitoring is commonly used for bearing fault detection.

questions.question: A machine health score below 60 generally indicates:
Why is real-time monitoring important in smart factories?

explanations.b: Real-time monitoring enables rapid fault detection.

questions.question: Which combination is most likely to reduce machine health?

explanations.c: High temperature and vibration significantly reduce machine health.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of predictive maintenance?

explanations.a: Predictive maintenance aims to avoid failures.
explanations.b: Predictive maintenance forecasts problems before they occur.

questions.question: Which technology enables sensors to share data with monitoring systems?

explanations.a: IoT enables connected devices and real-time data exchange.

questions.question: What should be done when abnormal sensor readings are observed?

explanations.a: Abnormal readings should not be ignored.
explanations.c: Correct action begins with identifying the root cause.

questions.question: Which Industry 4.0 feature is demonstrated in this experiment?

explanations.b: Sensor-based real-time monitoring is a core Industry 4.0 feature.

questions.question: What is the expected outcome when all sensor values remain within normal ranges?

explanations.a: Normal operating conditions improve machine health.
explanations.b: Failure is unlikely under normal conditions.
explanations.c: Healthy operating conditions produce a high machine health score.
